claim: Multi-agent orchestration is the next evolution - spawn specialized sub-agents from primary agent, each with own context window/model, working on task lists in parallel
actionable: Use task list pattern where primary agent creates tasks, spawns sub-agents (via Tmux panes/processes) to handle tasks in parallel, then collects results
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: Tmux panes enable visual multi-agent orchestration - each sub-agent runs in own pane, showing real-time status, progress, and context window usage
actionable: Use Tmux to spawn sub-agent sessions in separate panes when orchestrating multi-agent work - enables observability without external platforms
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: Agent sandboxes (E2B) isolate each agent's work environment - prevents interference, enables parallel execution, allows agents to make arbitrary system changes safely
actionable: Deploy agents to isolated sandbox environments (E2B, Modal, local) rather than shared systems to enable fearless agent action and prevent conflicts
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: Primary agent can orchestrate teams of 4+ Opus agents building full-stack apps - 160+ tool calls per minute across team, only using 31% context window on primary
actionable: Design primary agents to spawn specialized teams for complex tasks (e.g., 4 agents each handling a feature/app) - scales human effort across multiple focused agents
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: New Claude Code tools: team_create, team_delete, task_create, task_list, task_get, task_update, send_message - form complete multi-agent orchestration API
actionable: Use these tools to build orchestration logic: create team → create tasks → agents retrieve tasks → update status → send results → delete team when done
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: Multi-agent observability system tracks all tool calls, events, and inter-agent communication - critical for debugging and understanding agent behavior at scale
actionable: Implement event logging/observability for multi-agent systems to track team creation, task updates, tool calls, and agent completion - use to diagnose failures
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: The real constraint on agent capability is prompt engineering and context engineering (human skill), not model capability - models can do more than engineers know how to unlock
actionable: Focus on improving your prompt engineering and agent orchestration patterns rather than waiting for better models - current models exceed most engineers' ability to use them
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: Deleting agents after task completion forces healthy context engineering pattern - resets state, prevents context pollution, enables fresh approach on next task
actionable: Include team_delete in orchestration logic after collecting results - forces clean state transitions and prevents accumulated context from degrading future work
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes

claim: Engineers who understand agent orchestration can leverage agentic tech more effectively than 'vibe coders' - deep knowledge of system internals enables better prompting
actionable: Invest time understanding agent tooling, orchestration patterns, and observability rather than just trying prompts - deep knowledge compounds your leverage
source_title: Claude Code Multi-Agent Orchestration with Opus 4.6, Tmux and Agent Sandboxes
